summary: Filled in a substantial `src/melee/mn/mninfo` decomp slice, replacing several placeholder functions with C for the Data/Special info menu flow, text creation, scrolling input, model/proc setup, and cleanup. The header moved those routines from `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS` to concrete prototypes. decomp.dev reported `.bss` for `main/melee/mn/mninfo` newly matched at 100% and large partial match improvements for six functions, but the bot report shows most new functions were still not perfect matches.
key_files: src/melee/mn/mninfo.c - added globals, a local text-cursor overlay struct, includes, and implementations for mninfo menu initialization, scrolling, text rendering, model/proc lifecycle, and cleanup
src/melee/mn/mninfo.h - replaced unknown prototypes for mninfo functions/callbacks with concrete return and parameter types
what_changed: Added supporting includes for game/main, archive, audio, language, menu inline, scene/types, and HSD debug/GObj/proc/object/GX/memory APIs needed by the newly decompiled menu code.

Introduced `MnInfoTextCursor` with explicit padding to expose `HSD_Text* left` at offset `0x08` and `HSD_Text* right` at offset `0x18`, plus globals `mnInfo_804A0958`, `mnInfo_804A0968[0x48]`, and `mnInfo_804D6C78`.
Implemented `mnInfo_80251AFC` to initialize and sort the 0x42-entry `mnInfo_804A0968` list using `mnInfo_80251A08` and `gmMainLib_8015D804`.
Implemented `mnInfo_80251D58` to recreate a left-column `HSD_Text`, format date/time via `gm_801692E8` and `mn_8022EA78`, and switch date order based on `lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS`.
Adjusted `mnInfo_80251F04` to use `MenuInfo_GObj*`, free/recreate the right-column text slot, set font/fitting, look up text with `un_802FE3F8`, and emit it through SisLib.
Implemented `fn_80251FE4` as the menu input handler: cooldown handling, Back transition with audio/menu state updates, Up/Down scrolling through visible rows, row text cleanup, and row regeneration.

Implemented `fn_802523D8` and `fn_80252548` for menu-object lifecycle: transition cleanup when leaving `MENU_KIND_DATA_SPECIAL`, arrow visibility based on scroll bounds, animation updates, first-row population after `anim_timer`, model loading, GX link setup, and proc switching.

Implemented `mnInfo_80252758` to enter the menu, load `MenMainConSs_Top_*` archive sections, allocate/init `MnInfoData`, install GObj procs, create the description text, and create the separate input proc.
Updated `mninfo.h` prototypes for `mnInfo_80251AFC`, `mnInfo_80251D58`, `fn_80251FE4`, `fn_802523D8`, `fn_80252548`, and `mnInfo_80252758` from unknown signatures to concrete types.
smart_moves: Used `#pragma dont_inline` around small or sensitive functions (`mnInfo_80251A08`, `mnInfo_80251D58`, `mnInfo_80251F04`, `mnInfo_80252720`) to control codegen instead of letting the compiler reshape call boundaries.

Kept a narrow `MnInfoTextCursor` overlay with padding rather than overclaiming the full `MnInfoData` layout; this was useful for matching the repeated left/right text cleanup loops that advance by 4 bytes.
Preserved duplicated Up/Down repaint logic in `fn_80251FE4` instead of prematurely factoring it, which is a common matching tactic for menu input handlers.
Freed existing SisLib text objects before overwriting text pointers and nulled pointers during cleanup, matching the lifecycle pattern visible in the diff.

Sorted valid entries in two passes: first pushed entries with `mnInfo_80251A08(...) != 0` forward, then ordered valid entries by the value behind `gmMainLib_8015D804`, avoiding assumptions that valid IDs are contiguous.
Tightened public prototypes only after implementations supplied evidence for argument and return types, reducing `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS` in the header.
decomp_lessons: For menu screens with repeated text slots, lightweight overlay structs with explicit padding can be safer than renaming or expanding an entire user-data struct before all fields are known.

Menu decomp often needs both UI behavior and object lifecycle modeled: input proc, display/model proc, user-data allocation, HSD object setup, and cleanup when `cur_menu` changes must line up together.
Locale branches matter for matching and behavior; this code preserves US vs non-US date formatting through `lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS`.
HSD SisLib text objects should be treated as owned resources: call `HSD_SisLib_803A5CC4` before replacing slots and again when tearing down on menu exit.

Exact archive section strings are part of the match surface: `MenMainConSs_Top_joint`, `MenMainConSs_Top_animjoint`, `MenMainConSs_Top_matanim_joint`, and `MenMainConSs_Top_shapeanim_joint` were loaded directly in `mnInfo_80252758`.

Bot evidence shows this PR was progress rather than completion for most functions: `.bss` reached 100%, while `fn_80251FE4`, `mnInfo_80251AFC`, `fn_80252548`, `mnInfo_80251D58`, `mnInfo_80252758`, and `fn_802523D8` remained partial matches.
naming_conventions: Address-based names were retained for unknown/non-semantic functions and globals, e.g. `mnInfo_80251AFC`, `fn_80251FE4`, `mnInfo_804A0958`, `mnInfo_804A0968`, and `mnInfo_804D6C78`.
`mnInfo_` prefixes mark mninfo-owned symbols; `fn_` prefixes remain for callback-like routines without known semantic names.
The header keeps address comments such as `/* 251AFC */` while replacing `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS` with concrete signatures.
`MenuInfo_GObj` is a typedef over `HSD_GObj` and is used where the function immediately treats `gobj->user_data` as `MnInfoData`.
The local overlay uses padding fields named by offset (`pad_x0`, `pad_xC`) to document known layout gaps without inventing field meanings.
Many locals remain compiler/decompiler-style (`temp_r3_2`, `var_r28`, `var_r26`), indicating semantic naming evidence was still weak in this slice.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Applied `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop` around selected functions to preserve expected call structure.
Used `PAD_STACK(16)` in `fn_802523D8`, indicating stack layout padding was needed for matching.
Declared stack temporaries with explicit sizes and stack-like names (`char sp34[5]`, `char sp30[3]`, `datetime sp18`, `s16 sp16`) to reproduce formatted date/time and lookup call layouts.
Casted `MnInfoData*` to `MnInfoTextCursor*` and advanced via `(u8*) cursor + 4` to match pointer-walking cleanup loops over left/right text pointers.

Kept exact float constants for text placement and sizing, such as `3.45f`, `-5.8f`, `17.0f`, `514.2857f`, `142.85715f`, and `0.0521f`, which likely helped the `.sdata2` improvement reported by decomp.dev.
Used explicit callback casts like `(HSD_GObjEvent) fn_80252548` and returned `(s32) temp_r3` from `mnInfo_80252758`, matching the existing ABI/prototype constraints.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot report for GALE01 said matched data rose to 38.73% (+0.01%, +88 bytes).
Bot reported one new match: `main/melee/mn/mninfo` `.bss` +88 bytes, 0.00% to 100.00%.

Bot reported partial improvements: `fn_80251FE4` to 83.01%, `mnInfo_80251AFC` to 88.81%, `fn_80252548` to 86.69%, `mnInfo_80251D58` to 92.99%, `mnInfo_80252758` to 90.15%, `fn_802523D8` to 94.18%, `.sdata2` to 93.02%, `.sbss` to 50.00%, and `mnInfo_80251F04` to 76.88%.
follow_up_queries: mninfo fn_80251FE4 scroll_idx MenuInput_Up MenuInput_Down MnInfoTextCursor
